summary: O'Neil shares her excitement about volunteering at the Clearwater Festival, an environmental music festival founded by Pete Seeger in 1969 to support cleaning up the Hudson River. She describes the logistics of arriving early to set up camp before her volunteer shift in the kitchen. The post focuses on the personal pleasures of the festival: camping, jamming with bandmates, and discovering new music acts.
key_points: The Clearwater Festival was founded by Pete Seeger in 1969 as an organizing effort to clean up the Hudson River
O'Neil volunteers at the festival and plays fiddle with her band
The festival takes place at Croton Point Park and features camping for volunteers
She highlights performers she's excited to see including The Lone Bellow and The Felice Brothers
money_quotes: I know it's almost summer, not because of the sticky heat but because it's almost time to go to Clearwater
learning about new talent is what summer music festivals is all about
key_insight: A personal post about the author's participation in the historic Clearwater Festival, showing her life outside of data science criticism.
